content: Autonomous curiosity mechanisms from curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents can be repurposed to dynamically prioritize which unlabeled data gets stored and learned in state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems, creating a self-improving loop where the agent’s own innate curiosity drives memory curation instead of hand-coded sampling rules. This shifts active learning from a task-supervised data selection framework to an autonomous self-guided system that grows its own relevant knowledge base.
source_topics: active learning memory agent SOTA deep 2025
